title: hieracium tomentosum subsp. ragusinoides
intro: Hieracium tomentosum subsp. ragusinoides, also known as Ragusin's hawkweed or woolly hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium tomentosum plant, which belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Hieracium tomentosum subsp. ragusinoides include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant can be used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
